research_mapping.claim_limit: The proposal preserves a development-only diagnostic signal. It does not claim quality improvement, transfer, or held-out generalization.

integration_contract.stage_1: Run the existing primary development evaluation and freeze candidate IDs, objectives, feasibility, and code hashes.
integration_contract.stage_2: Only after the trigger gate passes, evaluate the frozen candidates once on fme_dev_order_regime_feedback_v1 and compile summaries through OrderRegimeFeedbackAdapter.
forbidden_direct_effects: change primary objective or selection score

all_required: The integration flag is explicitly enabled in a future separately frozen manifest.
At least two and at most four distinct feasible candidate code hashes are frozen at one controller checkpoint.
Every candidate has a complete primary development EvaluationResult and no timeout or failure_type.
The same candidate-set hash has not already been diagnosed under the same profile and implementation hashes.
The controller records a dev-only reason: aggregate-score near tie, suspected order fragility, or pending counterexample comparison.
The remaining evaluation budget covers every candidate symmetrically; partial candidate sets are rejected.
trigger_gate.near_tie_definition: Optional trigger only: absolute primary-objective difference between the best candidate and another frozen candidate is at most 0.01. This threshold does not alter selection or archive admission.
forbidden_triggers: candidate source, actor, provider, or prompt identity alone
post-hoc desire to rescue a losing candidate
availability of only one valid candidate

cost_contract.cache_policy: An exact successful key may be reused; failures are evidence and may not be silently retried or replaced.
cost_contract.budget_rule: Stage 2 is skipped when its full symmetric cost was not reserved before the checkpoint.

rag_memory_projection.retrieval_rule: Retrieve only for BP order-robustness queries or an explicit abstract strategy query about arrival-order sensitivity. Never retrieve this record as proof of held-out quality.
rag_memory_projection.promotion_rule: Promotion to transferable strategy memory requires a separately frozen confirmation or transfer experiment; this proposal cannot promote it.

rules: Do not modify the FME v2 manifest, profile, controller state, recorder, arm list, seeds, metrics, or held-out fields.
Do not reinterpret any existing FME v2 candidate or result using order_regime_feedback_v1.
Do not expose order_regime_feedback_v1 through the current runner CLI or any runnable manifest in this proposal.
Any future execution requires a new manifest, cohort ID, candidate-set hash, budget reservation, and development-only evidence path.
The optional adapter must remain behind a disabled configuration seam until a separate implementation milestone is frozen.

reject_implementation_if: Integration requires changing the primary candidate score contract, objective, or selection semantics.
The controller cannot prove symmetric evaluation of the full frozen candidate set.
Prompt or memory projection requires raw items, candidate code, held-out data, or absolute local paths.
The implementation cannot enforce exact cache keys and failure retention.
The FME v2 anchor hash changes before implementation begins.
disable_after_future_execution_if: Two consecutive separately frozen checkpoints produce no new behavior-profile separation, ranking flip, archive-admissible counterexample, or controller decision change.
Added stage-2 evaluation cost exceeds its frozen reservation or causes asymmetric missing coordinates.
The resulting memory is retrieved outside its applicability scope or is treated as held-out evidence.
Any data leakage, raw-sequence persistence, candidate-contract drift, or FME v2 contamination is detected.
exit_conditions.success_for_continued_optional_use: A future separately frozen comparison shows that the diagnostic changes at least one research_agent decision or prevents a repeated order-fragility failure while preserving full coordinates, budget, and isolation.

allowed_future_changes: Add a disabled integration configuration record.
Add a controller-side trigger and exact-key diagnostic cache.
Add a recorder projection from OrderFeedbackSummary to evidence and scoped memory records.
